content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660          322-5901           322-5641        322-5444

April 20, 1979

J. E. Thorne
P.O. Box 3673
Eureka, CA 95501

Re: Opinion Request
No. 79-005

Dear Mr. Thorne:

Thank you for your letter of April 3, 1979, requesting an opinion with respect to the provisions of the Conflict of Interest Code of the Department of Transportation. Since your letter does not raise a significant question concerning interpretation of the Political Reform Act, the Commission has declined to issue a formal opinion in this matter. However, I hope the following advice rendered pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b)1/ is helpful.

Specifically, you asked whether, as the director of a natural foods corporation and as a licensed real estate broker, you may negotiate a build-to-suit lease for a retail natural foods market with the owner of an unimproved parcel in the City of Eureka. In addition, you hope to rent from the state an adjacent parcel that will provide parking for the market. The parcels in question are in the right of way for the Eureka freeway. Presently, you hold the position of Chief, Environmental Planning and Project Studies for the Department of Transportation.

Nothing in the Political Reform Act prohibits you from entering into the business transactions described above. However, the Act does restrict your ability to participate

1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted.

J. E. Thorne
April 20, 1979
Page Two


in governmental decisions which may affect your financial
interests. Section 87100 of the Government Code provides:

     No public official at any level of state or local
government shall make, participate in making or in
any way attempt to use his official position to
influence a governmental decision in which he
knows or has reason to know he has a financial
interest.

     The term "financial interest" is defined in Section
87103 which provides:

     An official has a financial interest in a
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally, on:

     (a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);

     (b) Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);

     (c) Any source of income, other than loans
by a commercial lending institution in the regular
course of business, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value received by or
promised to the public official within twelve
months prior to the time when the decision is
made; or

     (d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management....

     Accordingly, entering into the business transactions
described above almost certainly would require you to dis-
qualify yourself from participating in any future decisions
regarding the Eureka freeway. In addition, you may not
participate in your official capacity in the decisions of
the Department of Transportation regarding lease of the
state property, nor may you use your official position to
influence any department decision concerning lease of the
property.

J. E. Thorne
April 20, 1979
Page Three


     You may also wish to examine the incompatible activities
statement for the Department of Transportation and also
Section 1090 relating to conflicts of interest in contractual
matters before making your final plans regarding construction
of the natural foods market.

     If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion request,
you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321.

     If you have any questions, please contact either Ted
Prim, Chief of the Conflicts of Interest Division, or me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Michael Bennett
                                        Executive Director

MB:KP
